title: LFS
text: LFS data can also easliy accessed across time. can now easily extract time series data, want perform many operations possible database level. plotting call collect, need done explicitly.  data efficiently ogranzied DuckDB, query runs quite fast despite explicit indexing database, taking less half second.  SEX variable recategorized GENDER concept starting 2011, older LFS PUMF data still uses SEX. can harmonize coalescing values.
code: lfs_pumf <- get_pumf("LFS", refresh="auto") unemployment_stats <- lfs_pumf |>    filter(LFSSTAT !="Not in labour force") |>   filter(AGE_12 %in% c("25 to 29 years","30 to 34 years", "35 to 39 years")) |>   mutate(jd=case_when(is.na(DURJLESS) ~ "Not applicable",                       DURJLESS<12 ~ "Less than one year",                       TRUE ~ "One year or more")) |>   mutate(Date=as.Date(paste0(SURVYEAR,"-",SURVMNTH,"-01"))) |>   summarize(Count=sum(FINALWT),.by=c(Date,jd,AGE_12)) |>   mutate(Share=Count/sum(Count),.by=c(Date,AGE_12)) |>   filter(jd!="Not applicable")   unemployment_stats |>   ggplot(aes(x=Date,y=Share,colour=AGE_12)) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~jd) +   scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent_format()) +   labs(title="Unemployment by duration of unemployment",        y="Unemployment rate",x=NULL,        colour="Age group",        caption="StatCan LFS (PUMF)") #> Warning: Missing values are always removed in SQL aggregation functions. #> Use `na.rm = TRUE` to silence this warning #> This warning is displayed once every 8 hours. microbenchmark::microbenchmark(collect(unemployment_stats)) |>    boxplot() #> Warning in microbenchmark::microbenchmark(collect(unemployment_stats)): less #> accurate nanosecond times to avoid potential integer overflows lfs_pumf |>    filter(LFSSTAT !="Not in labour force") |>   mutate(GENDER_SEX=coalesce(GENDER,SEX)) |>   mutate(GENDER_SEX=case_when(GENDER_SEX=="Female"~"Women+",                               GENDER_SEX=="Male"~"Men+",                               TRUE ~ GENDER_SEX)) |>   mutate(Date=as.Date(paste0(SURVYEAR,"-",SURVMNTH,"-01"))) |>   summarise(Count=sum(FINALWT),.by=c(Date,LFSSTAT,GENDER_SEX)) |>   mutate(Share=Count/sum(Count),.by=c(Date,GENDER_SEX)) |>   filter(LFSSTAT=="Unemployed") |>   ggplot(aes(x=Date,y=Share,colour=GENDER_SEX)) +   geom_line() +   scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent_format()) +   labs(title="Unemployment sex/gender",        y="Unemployment rate",x=NULL,        colour="Gender",        caption="StatCan LFS (PUMF)") lfs_pumf |> close_pumf()

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: pumf_locate_or_download() ensures version directory exists extracted content parsing begins. Cache layout: Decision sequence: refresh = TRUE — delete .duckdb file(s) metadata/ subdirectory, leaving zip extracted content untouched. Stages 2 3 re-run without re-downloading. redownload = TRUE — wipe entire version directory first, proceed first-time run. Implies refresh. Already extracted? — version_is_extracted() returns TRUE subdirectory (metadata/) non-zip non-duckdb file present. TRUE, zip step skipped even zip still disk. Download — URL looked list_canpumf_collection(). Surveys distributed via Statistics Canada’s EFT portal marker "(EFT)" instead URL; function stops instructions deposit zip manually. Extract — robust_unzip() handles two edge cases: Naming collision: zips contain single top-level directory name archive (e.g. 2025-CSV.zip/). colliding directory renamed strip .zip moved version directory. Encoding: older StatCan zips store filenames without UTF-8 flag (General Purpose Bit Flag bit 11). grep/sub calls zip entry names use useBytes = TRUE avoid “invalid locale” warnings.
code: <cache_path>/   <series>/     <version>/       <original>.zip          # retained after extraction       <extracted dirs>/       metadata/               # written by Stage 2       <series>_<version>.duckdb

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: pumf_parse_metadata() converts raw SPSS/SAS command files three canonical CSVs. function idempotent: nothing metadata/variables.csv already exists refresh = FALSE.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: detect_formats() scans entire version directory recursively identifies parser(s) apply. Multiple parsers can fire survey (e.g. SPSS split layout/codes SAS cards BSW weights). Detection case 5 also searches parallel French file — candidate set whose path includes /fran /french (case-insensitive).

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: Handles single-file SPSS format used Census (2001–2021), SFS 1999, SHS, others. file typically contains DATA LIST, VARIABLE LABELS, VALUE LABELS, sometimes MISSING VALUES FORMATS sections. VARIABLE LABELS optional (e.g. Census 2011 individuals omits ). Older releases like SFS 1999 DATA LIST label sections — produce fully importable table raw codes human-readable factor levels. Key parsing details: Column ranges — DATA LIST ranges may spaces either side dash (129-135, 129 - 135, 129-  135). three normalised regex (\\d+)\\s*-\\s*(\\d+) tokenisation. Record-group marker — leading / first variable line (e.g. /PROVP  1-2) stripped, discarded, variable retained. Section terminator — DATA LIST section ends first blank line, . line, occurrence VARIABLE LABELS, VALUE LABELS, MISSING VALUES, FORMATS, EXECUTE start line. keyword check reliable terminator older files (e.g. 1991 XMF) blank line DATA LIST VARIABLE LABELS. DATA LIST type annotations — () suffix column range marks character-type variable. parser records is_char flag per column uses populate variables.csv types VARIABLE LABELS section present. Sentinel detection — variables whose VALUE LABELS sentinel phrases (“applicable”, “Valid skip”, “Don’t know”, etc.) classified numeric missing_low/missing_high range, character. prevents spurious NA warnings numeric values fall outside label set. Zero-padded codes — unquoted SPSS numeric codes like 01, 02 normalised via .numeric() → .character() match bare integer values CSV data. Multi-variable VALUE LABELS blocks — /VAR1 VAR2 VAR3 headers (possibly spanning continuation lines) fully parsed listed variables receive code/label pairs.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: Used SFS, CPSS, similar surveys ship separate files variable labels (*vare.sps), value labels (*vale.sps), missing values (*miss.sps), layout (*_i.sps). layout_mask registry disambiguates single directory holds multiple sets (e.g. individual vs. household files).

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: .lay files supply fixed-width column positions; .lbe files supply value labels PROC FORMAT syntax. Variable labels come companion .sas file present. parser reuses parse_spss_split’s layout parser since .lay format identical.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: LFS ships single *codebook.csv one row per code value. Columns always read CP1252 regardless metadata_encoding registry field.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: Canadian Perspectives Survey Series ships variables.csv variable metadata ; layout codes. encoding defaults Latin-1 (CP1252 registry overrides).

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: Haven used binary .sav files text-format command file available. fallback surveys ship SPSS syntax.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: registry metadata_encoding field sets encoding text-format parsers. Default "CP1252" (superset Latin-1 correctly decodes Windows-era en-dashes curly quotes). Exceptions:

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: merge_metadata() takes list parser outputs produces single list(variables, codes, layout). Conflicts resolved: later parsers win duplicate variable names. layout present parsers, function checks every variable layout entry also appears variables, stopping diagnostic otherwise. final result written : metadata/variables.csv — one row per variable (name, label_en, label_fr, type, decimals, missing_low, missing_high) metadata/codes.csv — one row per code value (name, val, label_en, label_fr) metadata/layout.csv — one row per fixed-width column (name, start, end); absent CSV-format surveys

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: pumf_build_duckdb() reads canonical CSVs metadata/, reads raw data file, applies transformations, writes .duckdb file. function skips build target table already exists refresh = FALSE.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: find_pumf_data_file() searches version directory recursively. Extension pre-filter — derived registry file_mask: Several subdirectories always excluded search: metadata/, SPSS/, Command/, Syntax/, Layout/, SpssCard/, Reading_cards/, Documents/. Bootstrap weight (_BSW.) files also excluded; handled separately. multiple candidates survive, file_mask regex narrows list. one still remains, function stops message listing ambiguous files asks set file_mask registry.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: data file identified: FWF metadata/layout.csv exists data file end .csv. handles edge case (e.g. CHS) SPSS DATA LIST produces layout actual data ships CSV. CSV otherwise. paths read columns character (col_types = cols(.default = "c")) preserve leading zeros avoid premature type coercion. Numeric conversion happens explicitly next step.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: Registry data_fixups entries applied raw character data label mapping: str_pad — left- right-pad specified columns target width. Used zero-pad codes arrive without leading zeros CSV formats (e.g. SFS). rename — rename column; applied old name present (safe surveys ship multiple release variants, e.g. Census 2021 RELIG/RELIGION_DER).

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: registry bsw_mask + bsw_join_key + bsw_file_mask, BSW file found, read (CSV FWF), left-joined onto main data join key numeric conversion.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: apply_numeric_conversion() converts character columns typed "numeric" variables.csv: .numeric() raw character values. Missing range — values [missing_low, missing_high] become NA. handles SPSS-declared MISSING VALUES blocks. na_values fixup — additional raw string sentinels registry (data_fixups$na_values) set NA via trimws(raw) %% na_values. Used undeclared income sentinels older Census files. two mechanisms complement : SPSS MISSING VALUES block catches sentinels declared command file; na_values catches StatCan omitted command file documents user guide. Census income sentinel widths (confirmed SPSS DATA LIST sections): two sets kept separate: applying 7-digit sentinel 8-char field incorrectly NA valid $9,999,999 income value (stored " 9999999" trims "9999999").

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: apply_code_labels() maps raw character values R factors using codes.csv. factor levels complete ordered set codes table, just values present data. Unmatched raw values become NA warning shows first five offending values. lang = "fra", missing French label falls back per-row English label.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: labelled data frame written DuckDB dbWriteTable(). Factor columns stored DuckDB ENUM types. DuckDB ≥ 1.5.2 automatically; older versions ensure_enum_columns() runs ALTER TABLE ... ALTER COLUMN ... TYPE ENUM(...) factor column. separate DuckDB table created per language (table names eng fra, eng_<layout_mask> surveys multiple file types). write connection shut pumf_open_duckdb() re-opens file read-mode, preventing -process lock conflicts building language tables session.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: LFS handled lfs_get_pumf() (delegated get_pumf()). Instead one DuckDB per version, LFS versions share single <cache_path>/LFS/LFS.duckdb accumulating tables lfs_eng lfs_fra. Key differences standard pipeline: Schema evolution — new LFS version adds variable absent earlier versions, column added via ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN. variable changes type (e.g. VARCHAR → ENUM), ALTER COLUMN SET DATA TYPE used. Annual supersedes monthly — annual monthly versions year loaded, annual version supersedes monthly rows year. Version tracking — lfs_versions table shared DuckDB records versions downloaded parsed, refresh = "auto" downloads new versions. get_pumf() return — specific version requested, function applies dplyr::filter() SURVYEAR (SURVMNTH monthly requests) full shared table. Calling get_pumf("LFS") without version returns unfiltered table. label_pumf_columns() LFS — shared schema union loaded versions, variables introduced later years (e.g. GENDER added ~2020) absent older versions’ variables.csv. label_pumf_columns() therefore reads merges metadata every loaded version directory chronological order, -recent label winning conflicts.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: get_pumf() registers (series, version, cache_path, lang) package-level environment keyed DuckDB connection’s C++ external-pointer address: key survives dplyr tbl transformations select()/filter() calls operations create new connections. label_pumf_columns() uses .pumf_lookup_con() retrieve provenance; close_pumf() removes entry disconnects.
code: .pumf_con_registry  <- new.env(hash = TRUE, parent = emptyenv()) key = format(con@conn_ref)   # stable across R-level S4 copies

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: pumf_registry_lookup(series, version) returns named list controls every per-survey choice pipeline. Surveys without entry use auto-detection defaults.

title: Working with canpumf
text: take simple look share current renters forced move recent move, keyed revent move occured. estimate risk forced move look current renters move five years prior CHS, moved prior five years reners old accommodation, base ask share forced move given timeframe. also focus people move past 5 years, recall bias might make data longer timeframes less reliable reproted time windows get large.  looks interesting seem distinct periods frequency forced move changed, recognizing longer ago breackets conditional moved . PUMF data need aware dealing synthetic sample altered original survey repsonses privacy reasons. , general unvertainty dealing survey data, important assess good estimates . simple way add bootstrap weights, facilitated via add_bootstrap_weights function.  shows changes frequency forced moves fairly robust sampling issues. initial jump final reduction risk forced move interesting, final reduction may due strengthening residential tenancy act timeframe. Another complication interpreting data previous accommodation current renters forced move may different metro . CHS data location previous residence, note different city, metro area. can add variable see effect .  see frequency forced moves slightly elevated people moving within city. take digging data see might cause . One way contextualize compare Canadian CMAs.  shows patterns vary across cities, Calgary’s elevated rate forced moves four five year timeframe may due heated rental market boom phase time, rent hikes lack rent control may forced people move. data worth exploring , example vignette .
code: renter_chs_pumf <- chs_pumf |>   filter((Tenure == "No" &              `Previous accommodations - when move to current dwelling occurred` == "10 or more years ago + Always lived here") |             (`Previous accommodations - when move to current dwelling occurred` != "10 or more years ago + Always lived here" &                `Previous accommodations - tenure`== "Rent it")) plot_data <- renter_chs_pumf %>%    filter(`Geographic grouping`=="Vancouver") %>%   group_by(`Previous accommodations - when move to current dwelling occurred`,            `Previous accommodations - forced to move`) %>%   summarise(value=sum(`Household weight`),.groups = "drop") %>%   mutate(share=value/sum(value)) %>%   filter(`Previous accommodations - forced to move`=="Yes",          !grepl("10",`Previous accommodations - when move to current dwelling occurred`))   ggplot(plot_data,aes(x=`Previous accommodations - when move to current dwelling occurred`,y=share)) +   geom_bar(stat="identity",fill="brown") +   coord_flip() +   scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent) +   labs(title="Vancouver renters forced to move",        x="When move to current dwelling occured",        y="Share of current renters",        caption="StatCan CHS PUMF 2018") #> Warning: Missing values are always removed in SQL aggregation functions. #> Use `na.rm = TRUE` to silence this warning #> This warning is displayed once every 8 hours. plot_data <- renter_chs_pumf %>%   collect() |>   add_bootstrap_weights(weight_column = "Household weight", seed=42) %>%   filter(`Geographic grouping`=="Vancouver") %>%   group_by(`Previous accommodations - when move to current dwelling occurred`,            `Previous accommodations - forced to move`) %>%   summarise(across(matches("BSW\\d+|Household weight"),sum),.groups="drop") %>%   pivot_longer(matches("BSW\\d+|Household weight"),names_to="weights") %>%   group_by(weights) %>%   mutate(share=value/sum(value)) %>%   filter(`Previous accommodations - forced to move`=="Yes",          !grepl("10",`Previous accommodations - when move to current dwelling occurred`))   ggplot(plot_data,aes(x=`Previous accommodations - when move to current dwelling occurred`,y=share)) +   geom_boxplot(fill="brown") +   coord_flip() +   scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent) +   labs(title="Vancouver renters forced to move",        x="When move to current dwelling occured",        y="Share of current renters",        caption="StatCan CHS PUMF 2018") plot_data <- renter_chs_pumf %>%   collect() |>   add_bootstrap_weights(weight_column = "Household weight", seed=42) %>%   filter(`Geographic grouping`=="Vancouver") %>%   group_by(`Previous accommodations - when move to current dwelling occurred`,            `Previous accommodations - location of previous dwelling`,            `Previous accommodations - forced to move`) %>%   summarise(across(matches("BSW\\d+|Household weight"),sum),.groups="drop") %>%   pivot_longer(matches("BSW\\d+|Household weight"),names_to="weights") %>%   group_by(`Previous accommodations - location of previous dwelling`,            weights) %>%   mutate(share=value/sum(value)) %>%   filter(`Previous accommodations - forced to move`=="Yes",          !grepl("10",`Previous accommodations - when move to current dwelling occurred`)) %>%   mutate(`Location of previous dwelling` = gsub("\\.\\.\\..+$","",`Previous accommodations - location of previous dwelling`))   ggplot(plot_data,aes(x=`Previous accommodations - when move to current dwelling occurred`,                      fill=`Location of previous dwelling`,                      y=share)) +   geom_boxplot(position="dodge") +   coord_flip() +   scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent) +   labs(title="Vancouver renters forced to move",        x="When move to current dwelling occured",        y="Share of current renters",        caption="StatCan CHS PUMF 2018") plot_data <- renter_chs_pumf %>%   collect() |>   add_bootstrap_weights(weight_column = "Household weight", seed=42) %>%   filter(`Geographic grouping` %in% c("Vancouver","Toronto","Montréal","Calgary","Ottawa-Gatineau","Winnipeg")) %>%   group_by(`Previous accommodations - when move to current dwelling occurred`,            `Previous accommodations - location of previous dwelling`,            `Geographic grouping`,            `Previous accommodations - forced to move`) %>%   summarise(across(matches("BSW\\d+|Household weight"),sum),.groups="drop") %>%   pivot_longer(matches("BSW\\d+|Household weight"),names_to="weights") %>%   group_by(`Previous accommodations - location of previous dwelling`,            `Geographic grouping`,            weights) %>%   mutate(share=value/sum(value)) %>%   filter(`Previous accommodations - forced to move`=="Yes",          !grepl("10",`Previous accommodations - when move to current dwelling occurred`)) %>%   mutate(`Location of previous dwelling` = gsub("\\/town.+$","",`Previous accommodations - location of previous dwelling`))   ggplot(plot_data,aes(x=`Previous accommodations - when move to current dwelling occurred`,                      fill=`Location of previous dwelling`,                      y=share)) +   geom_boxplot(position="dodge") +   coord_flip() +   facet_wrap("`Geographic grouping`") +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") +   scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent) +   labs(title="Renters forced to move",        subtitle="(based on households currently renting that did not move in past 5 years or\nhouseholds that moved in past 5 years that rented their previous dweling)",        x="When move to current dwelling occured",        y="Share of current renters",        caption="StatCan CHS PUMF 2018")

title: Parse StatCan PUMF files
text: PUMF data can large cached locally. Set canpumf.cache_path option local directory via options(canpumf.cache_path="<local path>") .Rprofile. Without , data stored tempdir() session .

title: Parse StatCan PUMF files
text: PUMF data available StatCan via direct download can accessed directly via get_pumf(). cases, PUMF data must ordered via EFT deposited cache directory get_pumf() can find . get_pumf() downloads (needed), parses metadata, applies value labels automatically, returns lazy dplyr::tbl() backed local DuckDB database. Call dplyr::collect() load memory. Column values labeled automatically (e.g. province codes become factor levels like "British Columbia"). Column names remain short coded names default (e.g. PROV, LFSSTAT). rename columns human-readable variable labels, pipe label_pumf_columns(): done querying, release DuckDB connection close_pumf(tbl).

title: Parse StatCan PUMF files
text: LFS data organized year, except current year organized month. access data specific year: downloads 2022 LFS PUMF data needed, parses , loads labeled data shared DuckDB database, returns lazy tbl filtered 2022. access LFS data currently local database: ensure local database contains available LFS versions, use refresh = "auto". checks StatCan versions yet database imports :

title: Parse StatCan PUMF files
text: canpumf package supports Census PUMF 1971 2021. releases 1991 onward available via direct download; years 1986 earlier must ordered Statistics Canada’s EFT portal placed cache directory. default package loads individuals file. Available variants year:

title: Parse StatCan PUMF files

Versions marked direct download can fetched automatically get_pumf(); others must placed cache directory via Statistics Canada’s EFT portal. Additional Census variants (1986, 1981, 1976, 1971 — EFT ) registered specific parsing configuration yet end--end tested.

title: Generate bootstrap weights for a PUMF dataset — add_pumf_bootstrap_weights
text: **DuckDB-backed lazy table** (typical case), bootstrap replicate weights written directly DuckDB file separate table exposed persistent VIEW joins main survey table BSW columns.  returned `tbl` references view, downstream dplyr operations access every replicate.

title: Generate bootstrap weights for a PUMF dataset — add_pumf_bootstrap_weights
code: add_pumf_bootstrap_weights(   tbl,   weight_col,   id_col = NULL,   n_replicates = 500L,   prefix = "BSW",   bsw_table = NULL,   seed = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Generate bootstrap weights for a PUMF dataset — add_pumf_bootstrap_weights
text: tbl lazy `dplyr::tbl()` returned [get_pumf()], **** -memory `data.frame` / `tibble`. weight_col Name column holding survey weights (string, e.g. `"WSTPWGT"`). id_col Optional name column uniquely identifies row (DuckDB path ).  `NULL` (default), registry `bsw_join_key` used available; otherwise `pumf_row_id` added main table. n_replicates Number bootstrap replicates generate (default `500L`). prefix Column-name prefix replicate columns (default `"BSW"`). Columns named `prefix1`, `prefix2`, … bsw_table Name DuckDB table stores replicate weights (DuckDB path ).  Defaults `NULL`, auto-names `paste0("pumf_bsw_", tolower(weight_col))` separate calls different weight columns overwrite . seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. overwrite `bsw_table` already exists DuckDB file, overwrite `TRUE`; stop error `FALSE` (default).

title: Generate bootstrap weights for a PUMF dataset — add_pumf_bootstrap_weights
text: * **DuckDB path:** lazy `dplyr::tbl()` backed persistent DuckDB     VIEW contains original survey columns plus `n_replicates`     bootstrap weight columns, input `filter()` operations re-applied.   * **-memory path:** input `data.frame` / `tibble`     `n_replicates` new BSW columns appended.

title: Generate bootstrap weights for a PUMF dataset — add_pumf_bootstrap_weights
text: **-memory `data.frame` `tibble`**, bootstrap weights generated entirely memory augmented data frame returned. Bootstrap weights generated rescaled bootstrap: replicate sample \(n\) rows drawn replacement; bootstrap weight row \(\) replicate \(b\) `original_weight[] * count[,b]`, `count[,b]` number times row \(\) appeared draw \(b\). **Multiple weight columns (hierarchical data):** default `bsw_table` named `weight_col` (e.g. `"pumf_bsw_wstpwgt"`), calling function twice different weight columns (e.g. household weight person weight) produces two independent BSW tables two separate views without conflict. **Connection note (DuckDB path):** calling function fully shuts DuckDB -process instance held `tbl` (write connection requires exclusive access).  input `tbl` lazy tables backed DuckDB file become invalid call.  Use returned tbl instead. **Filtered input tbls (DuckDB path):** bootstrap weights always cover complete physical survey table.  `tbl` dplyr `filter()` operations applied, captured automatically re-applied returned VIEW tbl visible rows match original subset.  operations (`select()`, `mutate()`, etc.) replayed — interfere BSW columns — apply manually returned tbl needed. **ID column (DuckDB path):** stable row identifier needed link main table BSW table.  `id_col` `NULL` (default):   * survey registry `bsw_join_key` used available (e.g.     `"PEFAMID"` SFS 2016–2023) — table modification needed.   * Otherwise `pumf_row_id` column (DuckDB `rowid`) added main     survey table.  `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` O(1); `UPDATE`     fills values O(n).

what: Convert columns to numeric and convert all missing values to NA — convert_pumf_numeric_columns

what: Get a Statistics Canada PUMF dataset as a lazy DuckDB table — get_pumf
title: Get a Statistics Canada PUMF dataset as a lazy DuckDB table — get_pumf
text: Main entry point canpumf package.  Downloads (needed), parses metadata, applies bilingual labels, returns lazy `dplyr::tbl()` backed DuckDB file cache directory.  Subsequent calls reuse cached DuckDB without re-downloading.

title: Get a Statistics Canada PUMF dataset as a lazy DuckDB table — get_pumf
code: get_pumf(   series = NULL,   version = NULL,   lang = "eng",   cache_path = getOption("canpumf.cache_path", tempdir()),   refresh = FALSE,   redownload = FALSE,   read_only = TRUE,   ... )

title: Get a Statistics Canada PUMF dataset as a lazy DuckDB table — get_pumf
text: series Survey series acronym, e.g. `"SFS"`, `"CHS"`, `"LFS"`, `"Census"`, `"CPSS"`. version Version string (e.g. `"2019"`, `"2021 (individuals)"`, `"2023-06"`).  series single version omit pass `NULL`. lang `"eng"` (default) `"fra"`.  Selects set labels apply.  language creates separate DuckDB table (created lazily first request). cache_path Root cache directory.  Defaults `getOption("canpumf.cache_path", tempdir())`.  Set persistently `.Rprofile` `options(canpumf.cache_path = "<path>")`. refresh `FALSE` (default) reuses cached data.  `TRUE` clears DuckDB table metadata rebuilds already-extracted raw files (re-download).  `"auto"` accepted LFS downloads available versions yet database. redownload `TRUE`, delete cached zip extracted files re-download StatCan rebuilding.  Implies `refresh = TRUE`. valid `refresh = "auto"`. read_only Open DuckDB connection read-mode (default `TRUE`).  Pass `FALSE` allow write access, e.g. persist custom views derived tables DuckDB file.  Use [close_pumf()] release connection done. ... Accepts deprecated parameter names (`pumf_series`, `pumf_version`, `pumf_cache_path`, `layout_mask`, `file_mask`, `guess_numeric`, `timeout`, `refresh_layout`) warning.

title: Get a Statistics Canada PUMF dataset as a lazy DuckDB table — get_pumf
text: lazy `dplyr::tbl()` backed DuckDB connection.   Call `dplyr::collect()` obtain local tibble.   Call [close_pumf()] release connection.

title: Get a Statistics Canada PUMF dataset as a lazy DuckDB table — get_pumf
text: **Breaking change pre-DuckDB API**: function now returns lazy `dplyr::tbl()` instead tibble.  Call `dplyr::collect()` materialise local tibble. LFS treated specially: versions share single `LFS.duckdb` database.  Pass `version = "YYYY"` (annual) `"YYYY-MM"` (monthly). `refresh = "auto"` downloads every available LFS version yet database; valid LFS.

what: Get a write-access DuckDB connection to a PUMF database — get_pumf_connection
title: Get a write-access DuckDB connection to a PUMF database — get_pumf_connection
text: Downloads (needed), parses metadata, builds DuckDB, returns raw read-write [DBI::DBIConnection-class] database file.  connection gives full SQL access: create derived tables, persist custom views, join original PUMF data file. [get_pumf()] built top function adds language-table selection read-semantics everyday analysis.

title: Get a write-access DuckDB connection to a PUMF database — get_pumf_connection

Passed pipeline ensure labelled table language built returning. cache_path Root cache directory.  Defaults `getOption("canpumf.cache_path", tempdir())`. refresh `TRUE`, rebuild DuckDB already-extracted raw files opening.  **** re-download. redownload `TRUE`, re-download StatCan rebuild.  Implies `refresh = TRUE`. ... Accepts deprecated parameter names (`pumf_series`, `pumf_version`, `pumf_cache_path`) warning.

title: Get a write-access DuckDB connection to a PUMF database — get_pumf_connection

what: Get Labour Force Survey PUMF data from a shared longitudinal DuckDB — lfs_get_pumf
title: Get Labour Force Survey PUMF data from a shared longitudinal DuckDB — lfs_get_pumf
text: Manages single `LFS.duckdb` file accumulates downloaded LFS versions.  call either retrieves already-loaded data downloads, parses, labels, appends new version.

title: Get Labour Force Survey PUMF data from a shared longitudinal DuckDB — lfs_get_pumf
code: lfs_get_pumf(   version = NULL,   lang = "eng",   cache_path = getOption("canpumf.cache_path", tempdir()),   refresh = FALSE,   redownload = FALSE,   read_only = TRUE )

title: Get Labour Force Survey PUMF data from a shared longitudinal DuckDB — lfs_get_pumf
text: version LFS version string (`"YYYY"` `"YYYY-MM"`), `NULL` report database state return full table. lang `"eng"` (default) `"fra"`. cache_path Root cache directory. refresh `FALSE` (default), `TRUE` (re-parse re-label specified version cached raw files), `"auto"` (download versions yet database).  `refresh = TRUE` requires non-NULL `version`. redownload `TRUE`, delete cached zip extracted content specified version re-download StatCan rebuilding. Implies `refresh = TRUE`.  Requires non-NULL `version`. read_only Open DuckDB connection read-mode (default `TRUE`).  Pass `FALSE` allow write access LFS DuckDB.

title: Get Labour Force Survey PUMF data from a shared longitudinal DuckDB — lfs_get_pumf
text: lazy `dplyr::tbl()`, `invisible(NULL)` `version = NULL`   data loaded.

title: Get Labour Force Survey PUMF data from a shared longitudinal DuckDB — lfs_get_pumf
text: **Version types**: - `"YYYY"` (e.g. `"2023"`) — annual file released StatCan year-end. - `"YYYY-MM"` (e.g. `"2024-06"`) — monthly file current year. annual file year Y loaded monthly files year already database, monthly rows replaced (supersession). Conversely, annual year Y already loaded, requesting monthly year returns annual data filtered month without re-downloading. **Connection note**: returned `tbl` holds open DuckDB connection. Loading second version (.e. calling `lfs_get_pumf` holding first result) requires first tbl's connection closed first. Use [close_pumf()] `dplyr::collect()` result next call.

text: version_dir Path returned [pumf_locate_or_download()]. series Survey series acronym, e.g. `"SFS"`. version Version string, e.g. `"2019"`. lang `"eng"` (default) `"fra"`. layout_mask Optional layout mask; used DuckDB table name. file_mask Optional regex select data file.  Overrides registry. refresh `TRUE`, drop rewrite DuckDB table.

title: Build a labeled DuckDB table for a PUMF version — pumf_build_duckdb

title: Build a labeled DuckDB table for a PUMF version — pumf_build_duckdb
text: Skips re-building named table already exists DuckDB file `refresh = FALSE`.  Passing `refresh = TRUE` drops rewrites table without re-downloading re-extracting raw data. Returns db path table name invisibly.  Call [pumf_open_duckdb()] open read-connection get lazy `dplyr::tbl()`.  Keeping Stage 3 connection-opening separate prevents DuckDB file-lock conflicts building multiple language tables survey one session.

text: Named list elements:   - `variables`: tibble (name, label_en, label_fr, type, decimals,     missing_low, missing_high)   - `codes`: tibble (name, val, label_en, label_fr)   - `layout`: tibble (name, start, end) `NULL` CSV-format data

what: Open a DuckDB table as a lazy dplyr tbl — pumf_open_duckdb
title: Open a DuckDB table as a lazy dplyr tbl — pumf_open_duckdb
text: Opens connection `.duckdb` file produced [pumf_build_duckdb()] returns lazy `dplyr::tbl()`.  Use [close_pumf()] release connection done.

title: Open a DuckDB table as a lazy dplyr tbl — pumf_open_duckdb
Open a DuckDB table as a lazy dplyr tbl — pumf_open_duckdb
text: db_path Path `.duckdb` file. table_name Name table open. read_only Open read-mode (default `TRUE`).  Pass `FALSE` allow write operations DuckDB file (e.g. add custom views).

title: Open a DuckDB table as a lazy dplyr tbl — pumf_open_duckdb
text: lazy `dplyr::tbl()`.

what: Parse all metadata from a PUMF version directory — pumf_parse_metadata
title: Parse all metadata from a PUMF version directory — pumf_parse_metadata
text: Detects every parseable command-file format version_dir, runs applicable parsers, merges results canonical schema, writes metadata/variables.csv, metadata/codes.csv (optionally metadata/layout.csv) version_dir.

title: Parse all metadata from a PUMF version directory — pumf_parse_metadata
code: pumf_parse_metadata(   version_dir,   layout_mask = NULL,   metadata_encoding = NULL,   refresh = FALSE )

title: Parse all metadata from a PUMF version directory — pumf_parse_metadata
text: version_dir Path extracted version directory. layout_mask Optional string disambiguate multiple command-file sets coexist one directory (e.g. "CDN" Census); passed parse_spss_split parse_sas_cards. refresh TRUE, re-parse even cached metadata exists.

title: Parse all metadata from a PUMF version directory — pumf_parse_metadata
text: metadata_dir path invisibly.

title: Parse all metadata from a PUMF version directory — pumf_parse_metadata
text: Idempotent: skips parsing metadata/variables.csv already exists refresh = FALSE.

Look up survey registry configuration — pumf_registry_lookup

title: Resolve version aliases — pumf_resolve_version
text: Canonicalises user-supplied version strings.  Currently alias Census Population: bare four-digit year (e.g. `"2021"`) resolves `"<year> (individuals)"`.

title: Resolve version aliases — pumf_resolve_version
Resolve version aliases — pumf_resolve_version
text: series survey series acronym version raw version string supplied caller, `NULL`

title: Resolve version aliases — pumf_resolve_version
text: canonical version string (`NULL` `version` `NULL`)

what: Run the full three-stage PUMF pipeline for one survey version — pumf_run_pipeline
title: Run the full three-stage PUMF pipeline for one survey version — pumf_run_pipeline
text: Convenience wrapper : 1. Looks survey registry entry `(series, version)`. 2. Calls [pumf_locate_or_download()] (Stage 1). 3. Calls [pumf_parse_metadata()] (Stage 2) registry's    `layout_mask` `metadata_encoding`. 4. Calls [pumf_build_duckdb()] (Stage 3). 5. Returns lazy `dplyr::tbl()` via [pumf_open_duckdb()].

title: Run the full three-stage PUMF pipeline for one survey version — pumf_run_pipeline
code: pumf_run_pipeline(   series,   version,   lang = "eng",   cache_path = getOption("canpumf.cache_path", tempdir()),   refresh = FALSE,   redownload = FALSE,   read_only = TRUE )

title: Run the full three-stage PUMF pipeline for one survey version — pumf_run_pipeline
text: series Survey series acronym, e.g. `"SFS"` `"CHS"`. version Version string, e.g. `"2019"` `"2021 (individuals)"`. lang `"eng"` (default) `"fra"`. cache_path Root cache directory.  Defaults `getOption("canpumf.cache_path", tempdir())`. refresh `TRUE`, clear DuckDB metadata rebuild already-extracted raw data.  **** re-download. redownload `TRUE`, delete zip extracted content re-download StatCan rebuilding.  Implies `refresh = TRUE`. read_only Open DuckDB read-mode (default `TRUE`).

title: Run the full three-stage PUMF pipeline for one survey version — pumf_run_pipeline
text: lazy `dplyr::tbl()` backed DuckDB connection.

title: Run the full three-stage PUMF pipeline for one survey version — pumf_run_pipeline
text: stage idempotent: subsequent calls reuse cached results unless `refresh = TRUE`.

what: Remove bootstrap weight tables and views from a PUMF DuckDB database — remove_pumf_bootstrap_weights
title: Remove bootstrap weight tables and views from a PUMF DuckDB database — remove_pumf_bootstrap_weights
text: Drops bootstrap weight table(s) created [add_pumf_bootstrap_weights()] companion VIEWs DuckDB file.  BSW tables removed main survey table `pumf_row_id` column (added automatically [add_pumf_bootstrap_weights()] natural key available), column also dropped.

title: Remove bootstrap weight tables and views from a PUMF DuckDB database — remove_pumf_bootstrap_weights
Remove bootstrap weight tables and views from a PUMF DuckDB database — remove_pumf_bootstrap_weights
text: tbl lazy `dplyr::tbl()` returned [get_pumf()] [add_pumf_bootstrap_weights()]. weight_col Name weight column whose BSW table removed (e.g. `"WSTPWGT"`).  `NULL` (default), **** bootstrap weight tables (companion VIEWs) removed.

title: Remove bootstrap weight tables and views from a PUMF DuckDB database — remove_pumf_bootstrap_weights
text: lazy `dplyr::tbl()` backed original physical survey table   (without BSW columns).

title: Remove bootstrap weight tables and views from a PUMF DuckDB database — remove_pumf_bootstrap_weights
text: Like [add_pumf_bootstrap_weights()], function requires brief exclusive write access: read-connection backing `tbl` shut , tables dropped, fresh read-connection returned.

what: Remove a PUMF version from the local cache — remove_pumf_cache
title: Remove a PUMF version from the local cache — remove_pumf_cache
text: Deletes DuckDB table (optionally raw zip extracted files) one cached PUMF version.

title: Remove a PUMF version from the local cache — remove_pumf_cache
code: remove_pumf_cache(   series,   version,   keep_raw = TRUE,   cache_path = getOption("canpumf.cache_path", tempdir()) )

title: Remove a PUMF version from the local cache — remove_pumf_cache
text: series Survey series acronym, e.g. `"SFS"` `"LFS"`. version Version string, e.g. `"2019"` `"2023-06"`. keep_raw `TRUE` (default), keep raw zip extracted data [get_pumf()] can rebuild without re-downloading.  `FALSE`, delete everything including raw files. cache_path Root cache directory.  Defaults `getOption("canpumf.cache_path", tempdir())`.

title: Remove a PUMF version from the local cache — remove_pumf_cache
text: Invisibly `NULL`.

title: Remove a PUMF version from the local cache — remove_pumf_cache
text: default `keep_raw = TRUE`, DuckDB parsed `metadata/` removed; raw zip extracted data left intact [get_pumf()] can rebuild without re-downloading.  Set `keep_raw = FALSE` delete everything, freeing full disk space. LFS surveys DuckDB shared across versions.  Removing one version deletes version's rows; last version shared `LFS.duckdb` also deleted.
